title: Source-faithful Markdown and explicit LaTeX workflow
summary: Markdown and LaTeX buffers show literal markup with conceal disabled, while LaTeX gains explicit compilation, PDF synchronization, project navigation, diagnostics, completion, and formatting tools.
details: Markdown code fences and inline markup remain visible under ordinary navigation because both markdown_syntax_conceal and window conceallevel are zero.
Markdown, MDX, TeX, plain TeX, and BibTeX buffers reassert conceallevel zero when their FileType is established or they enter a window that may have carried another buffer's conceal setting.
VimTeX syntax highlighting remains active for LaTeX, but its command, math-symbol, delimiter, citation, and package conceal features are globally disabled.

Space-ll toggles continuous latexmk compilation; Space-lv opens the PDF or performs forward search; Space-lk stops compilation; Space-le shows errors; Space-lt opens the table of contents; Space-lc cleans auxiliary files.
VimTeX selects Skim on macOS when available, then Zathura, then Okular with source-position arguments, then MuPDF, then the system viewer. The current Fedora host selects Okular.
Texlab supplies completion, navigation, symbols, diagnostics, and explicit latexindent formatting. It uses a project marker when available and the source file's directory for a standalone document.
Saving does not start a LaTeX build and Conform does not format Markdown, LaTeX, or BibTeX on save. Explicit leader-f formatting remains available for LaTeX.

provenance.reference: lua/config/options.lua, lua/config/autocmds.lua, lua/custom/plugins/vimtex.lua, lsp.lua, and conform.lua
provenance.note: Current literal-source gates and LaTeX editing/tool lifecycle.

evidence.claim: Headless Neovim verifies inherited-window reset, unchanged source, visible Markdown fences and LaTeX commands on the real screen grid, VimTeX commands and described mappings, sole syntax ownership, and texlab's no-build-on-save settings.

evidence.reference: VimTeX official documentation at a5949d2800c1866c878956d49c51fc8d003c6b99 and nvim-lspconfig texlab definition at b386c50cd7816f8c8a2b13b298a218af1f5a75c6
evidence.claim: The plugin is intentionally non-lazy for inverse search; latexmk, viewer methods, conceal disable, mappings, and texlab build/formatter settings follow current upstream interfaces.

questions: How do I compile and view the current LaTeX project?
Does saving a TeX file automatically compile or reformat it?
Are LaTeX commands concealed as Unicode symbols?
What supplies LaTeX completion and diagnostics?

last_verified.method: Focused real-Neovim markup contract plus installed VimTeX and texlab interface verification.
